mnemonics: HOME-O-MORPHISM: Home means same shape. Two metric spaces are homeomorphic if one can be continuously deformed into the other without tearing or gluing, like a coffee mug and a donut.
keyPoints: A homeomorphism is a bijective function f: X -> Y between metric spaces that is continuous and has a continuous inverse.
If two spaces are homeomorphic, they are topologically equivalent; they have the same 'shape' and properties like compactness and connectedness are preserved.
Homeomorphism is an equivalence relation on the set of all metric spaces (reflexive, symmetric, transitive).
commonMistakes: Confusing homeomorphism with isometry. An isometry preserves distance, a homeomorphism only preserves topological structure. All isometries are homeomorphisms, but not vice-versa.
Assuming a continuous bijection is automatically a homeomorphism. The inverse must also be continuous for it to be a homeomorphism.
